description: Optimize email section loading speed when navigating between folders. Client-side folder caching, redundant API call elimination, query optimizations, and request lifecycle management.

userStories.title: Client-side folder cache with stale-while-revalidate
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire navigating between email folders, I want previously loaded folders to display instantly, so that I don't wait for a full re-fetch every time I switch back.
acceptanceCriteria: Switching to a previously-visited folder shows data in < 50ms (from cache)
Fresh data loads silently in background within 1s
Cache invalidates correctly when user archives/deletes/marks an email
Cache expires after 60 seconds (stale data protection)
Lint passes (npm run lint)
userStories.notes: Implemented stale-while-revalidate folder cache via useRef<Map>. Cache key: folder:source. TTL 60s. Fresh cache = skip network entirely. Stale cache = show cached + background refresh without spinner. Selection preserved on background refresh. Cache invalidated on: archive, delete, soft-delete, blacklist, mark processed/unprocessed, sync, realtime new emails.

userStories.title: Eliminate redundant fetchCounts on folder switch
userStories.description: As a developer optimizing API calls, I want to remove the redundant fetchCounts() call on every folder switch, so that we save 4 unnecessary count queries per navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Folder switch triggers exactly 1 API call (GET /api/emails), not 2
Current folder count updates correctly from the response total
Other folder counts still update via 60s polling
Lint passes (npm run lint)
userStories.notes: Removed fetchCounts() call from fetchEmails(). Current folder count now updated from response total via setCounts with folderKey mapping. fetchCounts dependency removed from useCallback array.

userStories.title: AbortController for request cancellation on rapid folder switching
userStories.description: As a user rapidly clicking between folders, I want previous in-flight requests to be cancelled, so that stale responses don't overwrite fresh data.
acceptanceCriteria: Rapid folder switching only renders the last-clicked folder's data
No console errors from aborted requests
AbortError is silently caught (not shown as toast error)
Lint passes (npm run lint)
userStories.notes: Added AbortController ref. fetchEmails aborts previous request before new one. AbortError silently caught (no toast, no console.error). Signal passed through EmailClientService.getEmails(signal).

userStories.title: Building Map for O(1) lookup in adaptEmail
userStories.description: As a developer optimizing rendering, I want adaptEmail() to use a pre-built Map instead of Array.find(), so that email adaptation is O(n) instead of O(n*m).
acceptanceCriteria: adaptEmail uses Map.get() instead of Array.find()
Building/lot display still works correctly
Lint passes (npm run lint)
userStories.notes: Created buildingMap + lotMap via useMemo. adaptEmail signature changed to accept Maps. O(1) lookup via Map.get(). Lot import added from types.

userStories.title: Optimize count queries — select('id') instead of select('*')
userStories.description: As a developer optimizing DB queries, I want count queries to use select('id') instead of select('*'), so that PostgreSQL query planner can use covering indexes.
acceptanceCriteria: All count queries use select('id') not select('*')
Counts still return correct values
Lint passes (npm run lint)
userStories.notes: Changed 8 select('*') to select('id') in counts/route.ts (4) and refresh/route.ts (4). head:true still used.

userStories.title: Optimize connections email count — head-only queries
userStories.description: As a developer eliminating N-row transfers, I want per-connection email counts to use head-only count queries, so that we don't transfer thousands of rows just to count them.
acceptanceCriteria: No full row fetches for counting — only head:true or RPC
Connection list still shows correct total and unread counts
Lint passes (npm run lint)
userStories.notes: Replaced bulk fetch + JS reduce with per-connection head:true count queries in Promise.all. 2 queries per connection (total + unread). Zero row transfer.

userStories.title: Optimize notification reply groups — SQL GROUP BY via RPC
userStories.description: As a developer optimizing SSR queries, I want notification reply groups to use SQL GROUP BY, so that we don't fetch all intervention-linked emails just to count them.
acceptanceCriteria: RPC returns top 20 intervention groups with counts
JavaScript fallback still works if RPC fails
SSR sidebar shows correct notification reply groups
Lint passes (npm run lint)
userStories.notes: Created RPC get_notification_reply_groups() in migration 20260225000000. SSR page.tsx tries RPC first, falls back to JS grouping. SECURITY DEFINER + GRANT to authenticated + service_role.

userStories.title: Scope sent replies fetch — exclude body_html
userStories.description: As a developer reducing payload size, I want sent replies to be fetched with limited columns, so that we don't transfer 200 full email bodies on every inbox load.
acceptanceCriteria: Sent replies don't include body_html
Conversation threading still works correctly (grouping by references/in_reply_to)
Email snippets still display for sent replies in conversation view
Lint passes (npm run lint)
userStories.notes: Replaced select('*') with explicit column list excluding body_html in getSentRepliesForThreads(). All threading headers (message_id, in_reply_to_header, references) + body_text for snippets preserved.
